topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my screen, a familiar, unwelcome intrusion. My stomach clenched instantly, a cold knot tightening beneath my ribs. It was a profile picture I hadn't seen in over a decade—a grainy, overly confident grin that used to make my palms sweat. 'Mark R.' The name hit me like a physical blow. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly before I finally clicked on the request. A wave of intense unease washed over me; why now? After all this time, this ghost from my past was trying to re-enter my digital space. I minimized the chat window immediately, focusing intensely on the texture of my desk, trying to steady the quickening rhythm of my pulse.

[story 2]

I stared at my phone, the glass cool against my fingertips. When I saw the incoming invitation, a sudden, sharp intake of breath escaped me. My jaw locked so tight I could feel the strain in my molars. It was him. That shadow from elementary school, the one who specialized in making others feel small. A flush crept up my neck, spreading across my cheeks. I felt a distinct urge to shove the device into the drawer, to erase the entire interaction. But curiosity, that insidious little worm, pulled me forward. Slowly, deliberately, I tapped 'Accept.' The silence in my room suddenly felt heavy, suffocating.

[story 3]

The sound of the notification was startlingly loud in the quiet afternoon. I snatched the phone up, my hand shaking so much I had to grip it with both hands. Seeing his name pop up felt like an unexpected physical jolt, a sudden surge of adrenaline flooding my system. My breathing hitched; I could feel the tension radiating through my shoulders, pulling them up toward my ears. *What is he doing?* The question echoed in my head, sharp and accusatory. I leaned back in my chair, crossing my arms tightly over my chest, adopting a defensive posture that felt utterly inadequate. I didn't know what to do next, only that I needed to find a way to regain control of the situation.
